summary: Iteration 10 — Share NFT Card (client-side 1080x1080 PNG generator). Frontend-only Playwright verification PASSED end-to-end against the live preview URL. All acceptance criteria met: (1) studio-share-card button visible to ALL viewers (verified as admin viewing a drop NOT owned by admin — Stormbreak Test, owner is a TEST user), label='Share', appears only when currentGen.status==='completed'. (2) Click opens share-nft-modal containing share-nft-canvas with width=1080 height=1080; canvas.toDataURL('image/png') length=1,682,430 (>>5000), confirming pixel data is fully rendered (brand 'BLOXDROPS · NFT PROVENANCE', edition pill '# 1/1', thumbnail of the drop, MYTHIC rarity badge, display_name 'Stormbreak Test', description 'Forged in the eye of the tempest.', 2 trait cards Edition/Element, MINT ID '0x3c57…8b5f', and live URL footer). (3) All three CTAs visible and functional: share-nft-download triggers an <a>.click() (download handler fires, no exception); share-nft-tweet opens https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?... with the proper share URL embedded; share-nft-copy clickable (clipboard call attempted; in headless write may toast 'Clipboard not supported' which is the expected fallback). (4) Modal closes via X (share-nft-close), Escape key, AND backdrop click — all three verified. (5) Regression: studio-edit-metadata still opens the NFT Metadata modal independently and the lock banner still appears on the seeded locked drop — Share and Edit Info modals are fully decoupled. The thumbnail branch (img onload success) was exercised; the fallback ◆ glyph branch is reachable via the same code path when thumbnail_url/source_image_url errors (verified by reading drawCard).

design_issues.screen: Share Card modal — rendered card body
issues: Minor: description text 'Forged in the eye of the tempest.' overlaps the bottom vignette of the thumbnail because cursorY starts at thumbY+thumbH+40 while the vignette gradient bleeds slightly past the rounded clip — readable but slightly low-contrast. Consider bumping the gap to +60 or moving description above the bottom-edge of the vignette.

Minor: there is significant empty vertical space (~240px on a 1080-tall card) between the trait grid and the MINT ID footer when there are only 1–2 traits. Could be filled with a subtle decorative divider or a QR code linking to shareUrl for extra virality.

test_report_links: /app/test_reports/pytest/it9.xml (regression backend reference — no new pytest this iteration since request is frontend-only)
critical_code_review_comments: ShareNFTCard.jsx line 7 — FRAME_URL is locked at module-load time via window.location.origin. If the component is server-rendered (SSR) or the user navigates between subdomains in an SPA shell, the URL printed on the card could be stale. Low risk in a CRA SPA but worth noting.

ShareNFTCard.jsx drawCard() — img.crossOrigin='anonymous' is set, but if the thumbnail server doesn't return Access-Control-Allow-Origin, drawImage will taint the canvas and toDataURL() throws SecurityError. In this test the FAL CDN served the image cleanly (dataURL produced 1.6MB string), but if a creator's thumbnail is on a non-CORS host, Download/Copy will silently fail with toast 'Couldn't export card.' Suggest pre-checking with a fetch+blob URL fallback, or proxying thumbnails via the backend.

ShareNFTCard.jsx copyImage() — Clipboard API requires a user gesture AND a secure context AND ClipboardItem support. On Firefox <127 ClipboardItem is gated. The catch block handles it gracefully (toast 'Clipboard not supported — use Download instead'), but consider feature-detecting (window.ClipboardItem) and hiding the Copy button on unsupported browsers instead of failing post-click.

ShareNFTCard.jsx — drawCard uses 'Inter' / 'JetBrains Mono' fonts. If the user opens the share modal before Inter is loaded (cold cache), canvas falls back to system-ui silently and the exported PNG won't match the live UI. Consider awaiting document.fonts.ready before drawCard().

ShareNFTCard.jsx — useEffect that runs drawCard depends only on [generation]. If generation object reference changes for the same id (e.g., parent re-renders with a new spread), drawCard re-runs unnecessarily. Use generation?.id as the dep, or memo the generation prop.

Studio.jsx line 678 — share-card button is rendered for every viewer when status==='completed'. Good (matches the spec 'visible to ALL users'). But there is no rate-limit or abuse guard — a malicious user could spam window.open(tweet) via repeated clicks. Low risk since it's their own browser, but consider debouncing the Tweet button.

ShareNFTCard.jsx onClick={onClose} on the outer div + onClick={(e)=>e.stopPropagation()} on the inner card — this works for desktop, but on touch devices a swipe-to-scroll inside the modal that lifts off-card will close the modal. Consider using onMouseDown on the backdrop instead so only true backdrop clicks dismiss.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all 6 acceptance criteria + close-via-Esc/X/backdrop + regression all pass

seed_data_creation: None. Reused existing seeded gen 6a3378223bc360a01b1ff640 (Stormbreak Test) — kept intact per iteration_9 handoff note. No DB writes performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Share NFT Card feature verified live. The /api/feed?sort=recent currently returns only 1 item (the Stormbreak seed); if you need a different gen, insert one or generate a fresh drop. Note: the share-nft-canvas is HTML5 canvas — Playwright cannot screenshot canvas pixel content via element.screenshot() reliably; use canvas.toDataURL() length as the integrity check (we used >5000 as threshold, actual was 1.68MB). Twitter intent and download were verified by intercepting window.open and document.createElement('a').click. Clipboard write generally fails in headless Chromium and that's the expected fallback path. The Edit Info modal (iteration_9) is unaffected.
rca of the issue: No bugs. Feature works as specified. Optional polish items captured under design_issues and critical_code_review_comments (CORS-tainted canvas, font-load race, debouncing) — none blocking, all defensible improvements.
